questionnaire.title: Fill out the personal questionnaire
questionnaire.description: Basic details for HR + payroll. Takes about 5 minutes.

story.title: Our Story
story.subtitle: From one Metallica t-shirt to a top 20% Amazon agency — globally.

milestones.title: EXPANDO is born
milestones.description: Adam Kurzok & Vojtěch Zogata found EXPANDO in Prague. First client: Metalshop. First product ever sold on Amazon? A Metallica t-shirt. The legend is real.

milestones.title: Growth through the storm
milestones.description: While the world paused, e-commerce exploded. EXPANDO helped clients thrive during COVID. The 'Happy Client First' philosophy was forged in this fire.

milestones.title: €100M+ annual GMV milestone
milestones.description: We crossed €75 million in gross merchandise value for our clients — and we were just getting started.

milestones.title: Global team
milestones.description: Teams across 6 countries. Clients in 7. Top 20% of Amazon agencies worldwide. 90 seller accounts in the agency, ~300 on the tech platform.

milestones.title: The next chapter — and you
milestones.description: Expanding into new markets, building AI-powered services. You're joining right when it gets interesting.

values.title: Our Values
values.subtitle: Five principles we live by — with real proof.

items.name: Always Interact
items.example: When one client's MRO products kept getting flagged for returns, we proactively switched to returnless refunds. The Account Health went green — without waiting for the client to ask.

items.name: Innovate with Data
items.tagline: We decide with data, not gut feel.
items.example: We integrated Intercom with an AI layer for customer support. Response time dropped to under 2 minutes. Not because someone said 'let's be faster' — because the data showed exactly where to act.

items.name: Do it Simply
items.example: Agency Support SOPs were scattered and inconsistent. We rebuilt them from scratch — clear, unified, easy to follow. The whole team aligned in days, not months.

items.name: Enjoy the Growth
items.tagline: Company growth and personal growth, together.
items.example: Crystal Bohemia wanted to try B2C for the first time. We made it happen on Amazon. The team learned, the client grew, and we built a new capability that now serves multiple accounts.

items.name: Happy Client First
items.tagline: The client's win is our win.
items.example: Institut Katharos had their Amazon account suspended. We reactivated it in record time — faster than any benchmark in the industry. They didn't just stay — they expanded with us.

services.title: What We Do for Clients
services.subtitle: Two core models, one mission: grow globally.
services.resellerDesc: We buy stock, list it, and sell it at scale. Think: a sports e-shop with 3,000+ products across Amazon, Kaufland, Allegro, and more.
services.privateLabelDesc: We build a brand's marketplace presence from the ground up. Think: a Czech cosmetics brand going international for the first time.
capabilities: Marketplace account management (Amazon, Kaufland, eBay, Allegro, TEMU)
Listing optimization — SEO, photography, A+ content

journey.title: How We Win with Clients
journey.subtitle: Five steps — from first contact to long-term partnership.

steps.title: We find the client
steps.description: Sales team identifies potential sellers, does market analysis, and closes the deal. Every great partnership starts here.

steps.title: We onboard them
steps.description: KAM takes over. Resell onboarding used to take 172 days — we cut it to 42. Speed is a feature.

steps.title: We grow together
steps.description: Example: Podpal went from 0 to 12,000 orders in 3 months, across 9 marketplaces. We treat every account as if it's our own business.

steps.title: We protect their reputation
steps.description: Customer Success keeps Account Health in the green zone. A suspended account is a crisis — we prevent crises before they happen.

steps.title: Data drives every decision
steps.description: Reports, dashboards, AI tools — everything we recommend is backed by numbers. No guessing.

day1.title: Your Day 1
day1.subtitle: Here's what your first day looks like. Come curious.
day1.miniTask: Throughout the day, notice one thing that surprised you from the client's perspective. You'll share it with the team at the end of the day.
day1.lunchNote: Team lunch — ask questions, break the ice

journey90.title: Your 90-Day Journey
journey90.subtitle: Three months, three gears. Here's how it unfolds.

months.label: Month 1
months.goal: Orientation, observation, and your first independent steps.
milestones: Shadow senior KAMs on client calls
Complete EXPANDO Academy modules for your role
Follow up on any outreach within 2 hours
months.output: Day 30 checkpoint: 10+ leads, 2+ solo meetings, fast follow-up habit

months.label: Month 2
milestones: Build and present your own pricing proposals
months.output: Day 60 checkpoint: 15+ leads, 3+ meetings/week, pricing confidence

months.label: Month 3
months.goal: Full autonomy. First deal. Knowledge contribution.
milestones: Pass EXPANDO certification (≥80% on weekly quizzes)
Write at least one Knowledge Base article
Contribute to team retrospective with data-backed insights
months.output: Day 90: CERTIFIED 🏅 + first closed deal + KB contribution

glossary.title: Key Terms
glossary.subtitle: The EXPANDO vocabulary. You'll use these daily.

terms.def: Fulfillment by Amazon — Amazon stores and ships the goods. Lower operational overhead, higher fees.
Fulfillment by Merchant — the seller stores and ships. More control, more responsibility.
The 'Add to Cart' button. Winning it = getting the sale. Determined by price, seller metrics, and fulfillment method.
Advertising Cost of Sales — (Ad Spend ÷ Ad Revenue) × 100. Lower is better, but context matters.
Total ACOS — (Ad Spend ÷ Total Revenue) × 100. Measures ad efficiency against all revenue, not just ad-driven.
Pay-Per-Click — Amazon's advertising model. Sponsored Products, Brands, Display.
Stock Keeping Unit — a unique internal identifier for each product variant.
Amazon Standard Identification Number — Amazon's unique product ID. Every listing has one.
European Article Number / Global Trade Item Number — barcode standard used for product identification.
Enhanced product description with images, comparison tables, and brand story. Increases conversion.
Amazon's dashboard showing policy compliance. We keep this green — always.
Order Defect Rate — returns, A-to-Z claims, chargebacks. Must stay below 1%.
When a buyer files a dispute directly with Amazon because the seller didn't resolve it. Serious.
Net Promoter Score — how likely is the client to recommend us? We track this quarterly.
Key Account Manager — owns the client relationship end-to-end.
The process of setting up a new client on Amazon and other marketplaces. Resell: 42 days. PL: longer.
Getting approved to sell in restricted Amazon categories (e.g., Beauty, Health). Requires documentation.
A product page on Amazon. Title, bullets, description, images, backend keywords — all matter.
Our model where we resell existing branded products. Identified by EAN/GTIN codes.
Our model for building a client's own brand on Amazon from scratch.
Objectives and Key Results — how EXPANDO sets and tracks goals quarterly.

practicalInfo.title: Practical information
practicalInfo.subtitle: Benefits, time off, and perks at EXPANDO

workingHours.label: WORKING HOURS
workingHours.body: Work is as flexible as you are. No fixed 9–5. Public holidays follow your home country (Czech in CZ, Slovak in SK).

vacation.label: VACATION & SICK DAYS
items: Mid-year start = pro-rated (e.g. starting in July = 12.5 days)
Roll over up to 5 days into the next year (max 30 total)
Approval via Vacation Tracker (login via Slack), then add to Google Calendar

sickLeave.label: SICK LEAVE — HOW IT WORKS
hppSteps: First 14 days paid by EXPANDO (60% of avg)
sickLeave.zl: As an OSVČ, sick leave is your own — covered by your private/voluntary insurance. ČSSZ kicks in only if you pay voluntary sickness insurance. Just let your leader know if you need to take time off.

perks.label: EXTRA PERKS
perks.homeOffice: 🏡 Home office — flexibly with your leader
perks.multisport: 🏋️ MultiSport card — 50% covered by EXPANDO (517 Kč instead of 1,034 Kč). Apply with Ema from day 1.
perks.payrollHpp: 💸 Payroll by the 10th of each month to the bank account from your questionnaire
perks.payrollZl: 💸 Invoices due within 7 days (see contract §3.6). Send them to fakturace@expan.do monthly.
perks.referral: 🎁 Referral Program — Bring someone awesome to EXPANDO! Get 10,000 Kč when they sign and 20,000 Kč after they pass probation (6 months). See open positions or ask Ema.

onboardingTask.label: ONBOARDING TASK (we'll do this on Day 1)
onboardingTask.body: Log into Vacation Tracker via Slack and ping Ema to assign you to the right department.

waysOfWork.title: Our Ways of Work
waysOfWork.subtitle: How we track where we're going — together

okrs.title: OKRs Updates
okrs.teaser: How we track where we're going — together

modal.whatItIs: Every quarter we set goals as a company. OKRs (Objectives + Key Results) are how we name those goals and measure if we're getting there. Numbers, not vibes.
howItWorks: Each team posts weekly — progress, wins, blockers
You see what marketing is doing, what tech is shipping, what sales is closing
All in one place, all in plain numbers
structure: Objectives — the goal that gets us excited (e.g. Happy Global client first)
Key Results — the numbers that prove we got there
Milestones — the checkpoints along the way
modal.exampleObjective: Objective: Use dynamic pricing as a default for our clients
modal.exampleKR: Key Result: Clients sell €1,000,000 worth of products through dynamic pricing
modal.exampleMilestone: Milestone: 10 clients agreed to a 5% average price reduction
modal.rule: "If it doesn't have a number, it's not a Key Result." — Marissa Mayer

allhands.title: All Hands
allhands.teaser: Once a month, we get the whole company in one room

modal.whatItIs: All Hands is our monthly company-wide sync. Every team shows up. Every team shares. It's how we stay one company, not five departments.
whenWhere: In the office — Praha or Havířov
Remote team: at least once per quarter in person
whatYouHear: OKRs update from every team — what shipped, what's stuck
modal.afterTheSync: The formal part wraps up, then we hang out. It's the easiest way to meet the people you'll work with — especially if you joined remote.

reviews.title: Performance Reviews
reviews.teaser: Honest conversations about how you're growing

modal.whatItIs: Not a corporate exercise. Not a checkbox. Performance reviews at EXPANDO are real conversations — about your work, your growth, and how you're feeling.
cadence: Quarterly check-ins — with your leader, on goals + wellbeing
First 90 days — weekly 1:1s with your leader
First 90 days — bi-weekly 1:1s with your buddy
whatWeTalkAbout: What you're working on and how it's going
Stuff that's hard — and what would make it easier
How you're feeling at work, honestly
modal.ourPromise: Your leader is in this with you. The point isn't to grade you — it's to make sure you're growing, feeling good, and have what you need to do your best work.
